senses.definition: a flat leather shoe without a separate heel, the sole of which is brought up and stitched to the top, originally worn by Native Americans
a leather boot with a pointed tip, raised heel and calf-high shaft, originally worn by cowboys
(trademark) a type of comfortable heavy boot with a laced-up calf-height shaft and air-cushioned sole
a type of shoe that is flat with a low heel, made of leather, and can be worn without fastening

examples.x: He slipped on a pair of comfortable loafers before heading out for a leisurely stroll around the neighborhood.
The classic penny loafer is a timeless style that pairs well with both casual and semi-formal outfits.
She wore suede loafers with tassels to the office, adding a touch of sophistication to her business attire.
The driver put on his driving loafers before getting behind the wheel, appreciating their flexibility and grip on the pedals.
The fashion-conscious man opted for a pair of brightly colored loafers to add a pop of personality to his ensemble.

senses.definition: a type of light athletic shoe made of canvas with a rubber sole glued to it
a woman's footwear that is open at the back and is fastened by a narrow strap
a slipper or a woman's shoe with no fitting around the heel
a light, soft shoe with a rubber sole, worn for sports or casual occasions
a rubber shoe that is flat and has an expanded front paddle that enables one to swim faster
a heavy leather shoe with decorative patterns and a hobnailed sole
a shoe with a set of small wheels attached to the bottom used for skating
an open shoe that fastens the sole to one's foot with straps, particularly worn when the weather is warm

examples.x: She slipped on her comfortable leather sandals for a beach stroll.
His flip-flop sandals left footprints in the sand as he walked along the shore.
The strappy gladiator sandals added a touch of bohemian flair to her outfit.
He opted for closed-toe sandals for a hike in the desert.
The colorful beaded sandals were handmade by a local artisan.

senses.definition: shoes with tall and thin heels, usually worn by women

examples.x: Walking in high heels takes some practice.
She prefers flats over high heels for everyday wear.
She wore high heels to the formal event.
Her red high heels matched her dress perfectly.
She switched from high heels to sneakers after work.

senses.definition: a shoe made of thick cotton or leather used for playing sports
a flat shoe made of canvas or leather with a rubber sole that does not slip and a lace going through a channel at the back
a type of shoe characterized by an open lacing system sewn on top of the vamp
a type of ice skate that is designed with a long, curved blade and a stiff boot, used primarily for figure skating and other ice sports
shoes that have tall and thin heels, worn by women

examples.x: The heels of her shoes clicked against the pavement as she hurried down the street.
She loved the feeling of empowerment that came with wearing a pair of stylish heels.
The actress towered over her costars in a pair of sky-high heels on the red carpet.
After a long day of wearing heels, her feet were sore and in need of a break.

senses.definition: (trademark) a boot with a line of small wheels at its bottom that is used for skating

examples.x: She wore her rollerblades to the park for a quick skate.
The rollerblades gave him better speed than regular skates.
He polished his rollerblades before heading out for practice.
She bought new rollerblades for her birthday.
The rollerblades were perfect for skating on the smooth trail.

senses.definition: a woman's shoe with a thick layer between the inner and outer soles
a specialized footwear designed for use in alpine skiing that provides support and stability to the skier's foot, ankle, and lower leg
soft and comfortable footwear worn indoors
a light, soft shoe with a rubber sole, worn for tennis or on casual occasions
a sports shoe with a rubber sole that is worn casually or for doing exercise

examples.x: He cleaned his muddy trainers after the hike.
The store had a wide selection of trainers, ranging from athletic styles to casual designs.
He prefers wearing trainers over formal shoes for everyday activities because they are more comfortable.
After a long day of walking, her trainers provided the cushioning she needed to avoid sore feet.
She wore her favorite trainers with jeans for a casual look.

senses.definition: a backless sandal held to the foot by a thong between the big toe and the second toe
a type of waterproof footwear made of rubber or other synthetic materials, designed to keep feet dry
a type of footwear designed to help individuals walk on top of deep snow
a woman's footwear marked by a thin high heel
a rubber-made overshoe that is waterproof and is worn in snow or slush
a type of strong shoe that covers the foot and ankle and often the lower part of the leg

examples.x: I love the sound of my boots clicking on the wooden floor.
I put on my boots to go for a walk in the snowy park.
She left her muddy boots at the entrance and put on slippers.
The fashionable boots added a trendy touch to her outfit.
The rain soaked through her boots, making her feet wet.

senses.definition: a casual shoe style featuring a white leather toe and contrasting color leather "saddle" extending over the arch of the foot
a pair of shoes made of leather with laces and a low heel
an ankle-high boot with a suede or leather upper, a crepe rubber sole, and two or three eyelets for lacing
a waterproof, typically rubber boot worn to protect the feet from getting wet in rainy or wet conditions
a military-style boot that typically features a high ankle shaft, lace-up front, and slip-resistant sole
a type of shoe with a smooth sole for sliding and a rubber sole for stability, designed for use in the sport of bowling
a pair of athletic shoes that is cleated at the sole, especially used for football
an ankle-high boot with two or three pairs of eyelets for lacing, typically made from suede or leather
a kind of shoe that is suitable for a range of activities and sports
an ice skate specifically designed for playing hockey, featuring a rigid boot made from a sharpened steel blade, and optional support features
a boot with a blade at the bottom used to move quickly on ice

examples.x: She laced up her ice skates and glided gracefully across the frozen pond.
He sharpened his ice skates before the competition to ensure optimal performance on the ice.
The children bundled up in their winter gear and headed to the local ice rink to try out their new ice skates.
They rented ice skates at the outdoor skating rink and spent the afternoon skating under the open sky.
Ice hockey players rely on their ice skates to maneuver quickly and smoothly across the ice during fast-paced games.

senses.definition: a type of military boot typically made of leather with a tall, stiff shaft that extends up to the knee or higher
a type of footwear designed specifically for horseback riding, typically featuring a tall shaft that extends up to the knee or just below
a type of women's dress shoe featuring a two-tone design with the vamp and heel cap in one color and the rest of the shoe in another
a shoe worn for running on a track that has spikes on the sole
a type of waterproof boot that are designed to be worn in wet or muddy environments
an athletic footwear designed for walking, providing comfort, support, and durability for extended periods of time
a shoe or sandal with a heavy wooden sole
a type of formal shoe characterized by its open lacing system, often with a rounded toe and a low heel, typically worn as dress shoes or for formal occasions
a backless sandal, usually made of rubber or plastic, with a V-shaped strap between the big toe and the one next to it

examples.x: She wore a pair of colorful flip-flops to the beach, enjoying the warm sand between her toes.
After a long day at work, he loves to change into his flip-flops for ultimate comfort at home.
The store had a sale on flip-flops, making it the perfect time to stock up for the season.
He accidentally stepped in a puddle, and his flip-flop came off, splashing water everywhere.

senses.definition: a type of shoe made for walking on rough terrains and long distances, often used for outdoor activities like hiking or camping

examples.x: He bought a new pair of hiking boots for the trip.
A good hiking boot provides ankle support.
She tightened the laces of her hiking boots before climbing.
His hiking boots protected his feet from sharp rocks.
The store sells waterproof hiking boots.

senses.definition: a type of waterproof footwear that extends up to the hip to protect the feet and legs from water and other hazards while wading or working in wet conditions
a rubber or plastic boot that extends to the knee and is waterproof, originally a military leather boot common in 19th century
a short, low-cut shoe that exposes the ankle, and is typically worn by children
a boot with a steel or reinforced toe cap for foot protection in hazardous work environments
an open and flat women's shoe, similar to a ballerina's shoe, usually worn for dancing or exercising

examples.x: She wore pumps to the dance class.
Pumps are comfortable for light exercise and casual outings.
The shop offers pumps in various colors and styles.
She paired her pumps with a flowing summer dress.
These pumps are ideal for dancing because of their flexible soles.

senses.definition: a pair of soft shoes worn casually or during exercise
